attacking_style: Launches solo attacks on final climbs with 8km to go, excels at high altitude. Capable of late-race accelerations inside 2km when in breakaway situations, particularly effective on summit finishes.
team_tactics: Quick-Step development rider with increasing autonomy for breakaway opportunities. Team allows freedom to pursue stage wins from breaks rather than providing dedicated mountain trains.
terrain_preference: High-altitude summit finishes (Cima Coppi, Tre Cime) where native altitude advantage materializes. Long-range climbs (8km+) suit better than punchy Monument terrain, though showing Monument podium capacity.
vulnerability: Still developing consistency in week-three Grand Tour scenarios. Monument terrain (Liège's punchy climbs) less natural than sustained high-altitude efforts, suggesting explosivity limitations in varied terrain.
key_patterns: High-altitude native advantage consistently materializes on Cima Coppi-category climbs (2 instances)
Breakaway specialist: both Giro stage wins came from long-range breakaway selections (2022, 2023)
Strong recovery capacity: won after 80km crash, matched accelerations across multiple climbs (2 instances)
Emerging Monument threat: Quick-Step investing in development, career-best Monument podium achieved
Long-range climbing attacks: 8km solo effort successful, comfortable matching early accelerations
form_trajectory: positive: progression from 22yo breakaway winner (2022) to Monument podium (2023 LBL) shows Quick-Step development path succeeding, stage win capability maintained
